background.inspiredBy: Recess and Everleaf both use relaxed negative space to feel elevated rather than clinical; this warm off-white keeps PEP premium, soft, and everyday.
background.name: Oat Milk
background.role: main website and label background — spacious, warm, non-clinical base

background.inspiredBy: Inspired by the low-clutter freshness of Recess and the approachable wellness world of BLOOM; it gives lifestyle wellness without medical blue-white sterility.
background.name: Spa Mist
background.role: alternate hero background, lifestyle content panels, social templates

background.inspiredBy: Draws from Poppi and OLIPOP’s treat-led flavour friendliness, softened for a more refined lifestyle feel.
background.name: Sunlit Peach
background.role: soft flavour-led background for peach/citrus variants, social cards, and warm product moments

competitorInspiration: Across Tier 1, the strongest lesson is that functional benefit should sit inside a taste-first, lifestyle-led colour world: Poppi, OLIPOP, BLOOM, and Recess make wellness feel easy through warm, optimistic palettes rather than supplement codes. Tier 2 brands such as Everleaf, Ole, and Ish show how restraint, premium spacing, and a few sophisticated accent colours can make a can feel like a lifestyle accessory, but PEP should stay lighter and more everyday than cocktail-led. Tier 3 brands clarify the boundaries: avoid the dark, high-contrast, technical, performance-coded palette logic of LMNT, NOCCO, HOLY, and energy/supplement brands, while selectively retaining their clarity around benefits.

primary.inspiredBy: Recess’ calm lifestyle freshness and Alive & Well’s approachable wellness cues support a cool, refreshing hero colour that is not sporty or clinical.
primary.name: Fresh Teal
primary.role: primary brand colour — can colour block, key borders, icon mark, primary website accents

primary.inspiredBy: Everleaf and Yaya Kombucha show how warm natural neutrals can make wellness feel crafted and premium; this keeps PEP gentle rather than supplement-like.
primary.name: Warm Cream
primary.role: signature neutral — label field, editorial backgrounds, premium whitespace

primary.inspiredBy: Poppi, BLOOM, and OLIPOP use fruit-forward warmth to make better-for-you drinks feel like treats; this coral brings appetite and friendliness.
primary.name: Soft Coral
primary.role: warm accent — CTA buttons, flavour highlights, social stickers, small callouts

rejectPatterns: Neon green, black, silver, and electric blue combinations that imply energy drink or gym performance.
Clinical white-and-medical-blue systems with dense benefit grids or laboratory credibility cues.
Heavy black backgrounds, metallic gradients, flames, lightning, or aggressive contrast associated with sports nutrition.
Overly childish rainbow soda palettes with hard candy saturation and bubblegum graphics.
Formal dark spirits-bar palettes that make PEP feel like an alcohol replacement rather than an everyday refreshment.

secondary.inspiredBy: OLIPOP and Poppi prove that better-for-you can still feel sunny and treat-like; this is a restrained, premium yellow rather than candy yellow.
secondary.name: Apricot Fizz
secondary.role: secondary flavour accent — highlights, small badges, lifestyle warmth

secondary.inspiredBy: Recess and more fiz suggest light blue can communicate sparkling refreshment when softened and paired with warm neutrals.
secondary.name: Mist Blue
secondary.role: secondary cool accent — hydration cues, background blocks, subtle product shadows

secondary.inspiredBy: Alive & Well and Yaya Kombucha use natural freshness cues; this soft green adds clean wellness credibility without botanical illustration.
secondary.name: Cucumber Glass
secondary.role: secondary freshness accent — flavour system, divider shapes, small graphic fills

text.inspiredBy: Everleaf and Walker Brothers demonstrate that premium beverage identity benefits from warm dark text instead of harsh black.
text.name: Deep Espresso
text.role: primary text — wordmark, headings, high-contrast body copy

text.inspiredBy: Recess-style calm editorial design calls for a softer secondary text tone that preserves readability without looking technical.
text.name: Slate Cocoa
text.role: secondary text — captions, supporting claims, product facts, menu descriptions

text.inspiredBy: Borrowed from premium lifestyle beverage systems where reversed type feels clean and understated on deeper accent blocks.
text.name: White Foam
text.role: reversed text — CTA text, dark panels, colour-blocked label details
